what_it_does: Acceptance test for the swappable STT ear registry and the whisper.cpp local_http provider. It runs four by-use checks: available_stt() is a no-raise status read with legible detail; whisper.cpp transcribes a real audio clip via /v1/audio/transcriptions; every registered provider id dispatches and back-compat aliases resolve; and a selected-but-down local_http provider raises loudly without falling back.
observations: Uses live external services on fixed ports (:2022 whisper.cpp, :4123 Kokoro TTS).
If the live whisper.cpp ear is unreachable, check (2) is skipped with a loud notice and the run exits non-zero.
Generates a silent WAV for routing checks that must not depend on a live ear.
Fetches a real spoken clip from Kokoro TTS so the transcription check is by-use, not fabricated.
Monkeypatches STT_PROVIDERS with a transient dead local_http provider to test fail-loud behavior, then deletes it in a finally block.
Documents why it uses /v1/audio/transcriptions instead of /inference based on empirical testing of the running build.
Maintains global PASS and ok counters and exits 0/1 accordingly.
standouts: Explicit live-endpoint policy: skip loud rather than vacuous pass.
Deliberately dead-provider probe to prove no silent fallback.
Comments treat the file as a design contract, explaining evidence-over-spec route choice.
conventions: Docstring states purpose, policy, and run command up front.
Uses sys.path insertion to import from the repo root.
Environment variable override COMPANY_TTS_URL for the TTS bridge.
Back-compat checks for aliases and legacy names (available(), DEFAULT_PROVIDER).
concerns: Hard-coded service ports and external dependencies make the test environment-sensitive.
Monkeypatching the global STT_PROVIDERS catalog is file-local but could leak state if an exception escapes the finally.
Routing checks catch broad Exception and treat any non-'unknown' error as success, which may mask real bugs.
Global mutable PASS/ok counters are not thread-safe.
notes: A live-service acceptance script that treats the STT registry as a swappable ear and verifies status, transcription, dispatch, aliases, and loud failure in one executable contract.
questions: Where are the alias mappings (whisper/local/whisper_local) defined in the voice.stt module?
What is the exact schema of STT_PROVIDERS entries?
How is active_ear() selected and does it honor environment overrides?
Is there a CI mode that stubs the live services so this can run without :2022/:4123?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the executed checks: status read, live transcription, dispatch/aliases, and fail-loud no-fallback.
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce that the STT subsystem behaves like a pluggable ear where the catalog is the single source of truth, selection works by id and alias, real transcription works against the actual whisper.cpp build, and a down selected provider errors visibly rather than silently switching ears.
novelty: It documents an empirically discovered route (/v1/audio/transcriptions) and couples the STT acceptance test to a live Kokoro TTS clip so transcription is proven by actual use rather than a canned sample.
contribution: An executable contract that captures the expected runtime semantics of the STT provider registry: non-raising status enumeration, live whisper.cpp transcription, canonical-id and alias dispatch, and loud failure on unreachable local_http providers.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test script that verifies the voice STT provider registry status, whisper.cpp live transcription, provider id/alias dispatch, and loud no-fallback failure for a down local_http ear.
intention_for_embedding: Validate the swappable STT ear implementation against real services and deliberately dead-provider scenarios without silent fallbacks.

symbols.does: Records a pass/fail check and updates global counters.
symbols.description: A simple test helper that increments PASS and prints PASS when cond is true; otherwise sets ok to False and prints FAIL.

symbols.does: Generates a valid silent mono WAV in memory.
symbols.description: Creates a 16 kHz 16-bit mono WAV using the wave module, used as dummy audio for routing and no-fallback checks that do not require a live ear.

symbols.does: Fetches a real spoken audio clip from the Kokoro TTS bridge.
symbols.description: POSTs a sentence to the TTS endpoint (default http://127.0.0.1:4123/tts) and returns the audio bytes, or None if the TTS service is unreachable so the transcription check can skip loudly.

symbols.does: Probes whether the whisper.cpp HTTP ear is reachable.
symbols.description: Opens http://127.0.0.1:2022/ with a short timeout and returns True only if the response status is 200, used to decide whether the live transcription check can run.
